meta.title: Software Architect & Full-Stack Engineer | SAIFCORE
meta.description: Software architect & full-stack engineer based in Dakar, Senegal — scalable systems for fintech and SaaS. Open to national and international collaboration.
meta.ogDescription: Scalable architecture, SaaS systems, and fintech integrations — built for the real world.
meta.twitterDescription: Scalable systems, SaaS platforms, and fintech integrations.
meta.jsonLdDescription: Software architect and full-stack engineer based in Dakar, Senegal. Designs scalable systems for fintech and SaaS; open to remote and hybrid collaboration internationally.
meta.manifestDescription: Scalable systems, SaaS platforms, and fintech integrations — built for the real world.

hero.subtitle: I design scalable systems that solve real business problems — from fintech to SaaS platforms.
hero.locationLine: Based in Dakar, Senegal — available for on-site, hybrid, and remote work across Senegal, Francophone Africa, and internationally (EU/US overlap friendly).
hero.nextSteps: Typical path: short intro call → written scope & constraints → proposal or kickoff. No endless back-and-forth.
hero.jumpToContactHint: email, CV, and full booking options.

trust.heading: Trusted by companies and real-world projects

whatISolve.title: What I solve
whatISolve.subtitle: From first principles to production — architecture that aligns with your business constraints.

items.title: Scalable Architecture
items.description: Domain-driven boundaries, clear contracts, and patterns that keep teams shipping as complexity grows.

items.title: SaaS Systems
items.description: Multi-tenant models, billing hooks, and operational tooling built for long-term product velocity.

items.title: Payment Integration
items.description: Secure money movement, reconciliation, and compliance-aware flows across regions and providers.

items.title: Performance Optimization
items.description: Latency budgets, caching strategy, and observability so systems stay fast under real load.

howIThink.title: How I think
howIThink.subtitle: A disciplined loop from problem to resilient systems — no unnecessary ceremony.

steps.title: Understand business problem
steps.body: Stakeholders, constraints, and success metrics before touching tech.

steps.title: Design architecture
steps.body: Clear boundaries, interfaces, and evolution paths your team can own.

steps.title: Build scalable systems
steps.body: Pragmatic delivery with testing, CI, and operational guardrails.

steps.title: Optimize for real-world usage
steps.body: Measure, tune, and harden where it matters for users and revenue.

howIThink.operatingBody: Architecture is a product: documented decisions, measurable outcomes, and room for your team to evolve the system without heroics.
bullets: Clear ADRs and diagrams where they reduce risk
SLOs, tracing, and alerts that match user journeys
Security and compliance baked in, not bolted on

featuredProjects.title: Featured projects
featuredProjects.subtitle: Selected work — including Ecom 360 PME, plus anonymized engagements where needed. Impact over vanity metrics.
featuredProjects.bridgeSubtitle: Tell me about your constraints — I’ll suggest a realistic architecture and delivery path.

items.title: Ecom 360 PME
items.context: SaaS platform for retail and commerce management — inventory, sales, and day-to-day operations in one product built to help SMEs scale.
items.impact: Guided onboarding and a focused UX so merchants adopt the system without a heavy IT project.

items.title: Multi-tenant B2B SaaS
items.context: Greenfield platform with org isolation, role-based access, and usage-based billing.
items.impact: 40% faster feature cycle via modular domains

items.title: Cross-border payments hub
items.context: Unified orchestration across PSPs with reconciliation and audit trails.
items.impact: Sub-second auth path at peak settlement windows

items.title: Realtime analytics pipeline
items.context: Event ingestion, enrichment, and dashboards for product and finance teams.
items.impact: Same-day insights vs. weekly batch exports

architectureExpertise.title: Architecture expertise
architectureExpertise.subtitle: Depth where systems meet business — not buzzwords for slides.

about.title: About
about.p1: I'm a software architect and full-stack engineer focused on systems that survive contact with reality — regulation, flaky networks, and teams that grow faster than documentation.
about.p2c: , explicit trade-offs, and architectures your future self will thank you for.
about.p3: I've shipped across fintech and SaaS, partnering with founders and enterprise teams to turn ambitious roadmaps into dependable platforms.
about.p4: I work with teams in Senegal and abroad: async-first communication, clear written specs, and scheduled overlap when decisions need real-time alignment.

insights.title: Insights & thinking
insights.subtitle: Notes on building serious software in serious environments.
insights.endCtaTitle: Facing a similar challenge in your stack?
insights.endCtaSubtitle: These articles are a starting point — the next step is a concrete conversation about your context.

articlesPage.metaDescription: Writing on architecture, SaaS, and fintech — practical notes for serious software.
articlesPage.title: Articles
articlesPage.subtitle: Articles, code samples, ADRs, design notes, and document outlines — all in one place.
articlesPage.endCtaTitle: Want to apply this to your product?
articlesPage.endCtaSubtitle: Share your stack, constraints, and timeline — I’ll say clearly if there’s a fit.

cta.title: Let's design your next system.
cta.subtitle: Tell me about your product, constraints, and timeline — I'll reply with a clear path forward.
cta.processStep1: You book a slot or send a brief — context, goals, and timeline.
cta.processStep2: I come back with clarifying questions and, when it fits, a lean scope or options.
cta.processStep3: We align on the next step: discovery workshop, architecture sprint, or phased delivery.
cta.responseHint: I usually acknowledge messages within two business days (often faster).
